content.content:
# SEO Rules

Full reference infrastructure lives in:
- `apps/web/src/lib/seo.ts` — siteConfig, 19 schema generators, `generatePageMetadata()`
- `apps/web/src/utils/seoUtils.ts` — blog/use-case metadata helpers
- `apps/web/src/lib/sitemapData.ts` — 11-part sitemap architecture
- `apps/web/src/i18n/getAlternates.ts` — hreflang helpers

---

## Page Titles

The root layout (`apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx`) defines:

```typescript
title: { template: `%s | GAIA` }
```

**Never include "GAIA" or "| GAIA" in a page's own title string** — it renders as `Page | GAIA | GAIA`.

```typescript
// Wrong — "Features — GAIA | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "Features — GAIA" };

// Wrong — "AI Chief of Staff | GAIA | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "AI Chief of Staff | GAIA" };

// Correct — "Features | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "Features" };

// Correct — "AI Chief of Staff — Your Proactive AI | GAIA"
export const metadata = { title: "AI Chief of Staff — Your Proactive AI" };
```

This applies to both static `export const metadata` objects and dynamic `generateMetadata()` functions — strip `| GAIA` from any returned title string.

**Title formatting:**
- Lead with the most descriptive keyword, not the brand name
- Use em-dash `—` (not pipe `|`) to separate primary topic from subtitle
- Keep under 60 characters where possible
- No trailing punctuation

---

## Meta Descriptions

- 120–155 characters
- Describe what the page *does*, not what the product *is*
- Do not repeat the title verbatim
- End with a benefit or differentiator

---

## Always Use `generatePageMetadata()` for New Pages

`lib/seo.ts` exports `generatePageMetadata()` — use it instead of building metadata objects by hand. It handles OG, Twitter, canonical, noIndex, article timestamps, and authors in one call:

```typescript
import { generatePageMetadata } from "@/lib/seo";

export async function generateMetadata(): Promise<Metadata> {
  return generatePageMetadata({
    title: "My Page Title",
    description: "What this page does in 120–155 chars.",
    path: "/my-page",
    keywords: ["relevant", "keyword", "list"],
  });
}
```

Never construct `openGraph` or `twitter` metadata blocks manually — `generatePageMetadata()` already does this correctly and consistently.

---

## Structured Data (JSON-LD)

`lib/seo.ts` exports 19 typed schema generators. Use them — never write raw JSON-LD objects inline.

| Need | Generator |
|---|---|
| Any new page | `generateWebPageSchema()` |
| FAQ section | `generateFAQSchema()` + render `<FAQAccordion>` |
| How-to / steps | `generateHowToSchema()` |
| Blog post | `generateArticleSchema()` |
| List of items | `generateItemListSchema()` |
| Glossary term | `generateDefinedTermSchema()` |
| Product/app | `generateProductSchema()` |
| Breadcrumbs | `generateBreadcrumbSchema()` |

Render schemas with `<JsonLd>` from `components/seo/JsonLd.tsx`. Every programmatic page should have at minimum a `WebPageSchema` and a `BreadcrumbSchema`.

```typescript
import JsonLd from "@/components/seo/JsonLd";
import { generateBreadcrumbSchema, generateWebPageSchema } from "@/lib/seo";

// In the page component (Server Component):
<JsonLd
  schema={[
    generateWebPageSchema({ title, description, url }),
    generateBreadcrumbSchema([
      { name: "Home", url: "/" },
      { name: "Features", url: "/features" },
      { name: feature.title, url: `/features/${slug}` },
    ]),
  ]}
/>
```

---

## Programmatic SEO Pages (`generateStaticParams` + `generateMetadata`)

Every `[slug]` route must implement both:

```typescript
export async function generateStaticParams() {
  const items = await fetchAllItems();
  return items.map((item) => ({ slug: item.slug }));
}

export async function generateMetadata({ params }): Promise<Metadata> {
  const item = await getItem(params.slug);
  if (!item) return {};
  return generatePageMetadata({
    title: item.title,
    description: item.description,
    path: `/section/${params.slug}`,
    keywords: [item.name, item.category, ...commonKeywords],
  });
}
```

**For integration/marketplace pages:**
- Use `revalidate = 60` (ISR) since content is API-driven
- Set `dynamicParams = true` to allow fallback rendering for new slugs
- Generate category-specific title and description templates (see `marketplace/[slug]/page.tsx` for the pattern)
- Always include a `SoftwareApplication` schema, HowTo schema, and FAQ schema

**Canonical URLs:**
- Use `getCanonicalUrl(path)` from `lib/seo.ts` — never construct URLs manually
- For near-duplicate pages (glossary terms, synonym slugs), set `canonicalPath` to the primary slug

---

## Hreflang / Internationalization

Translated pages (anything under `[locale]/`) that have content in all 7 locales must include `alternates.languages`:

```typescript
import { getAlternates } from "@/i18n/getAlternates";

export async function generateMetadata({ params }) {
  return {
    ...generatePageMetadata({ title, description, path }),
    alternates: {
      canonical: `/my-page`,
      languages: getAlternates("/my-page"),
    },
  };
}
```

**Translated (needs hreflang):** `/compare`, `/alternative-to`, `/automate`, `/for`, `/learn`, `/use-cases`

**Untranslated (English only — no hreflang):** `/marketplace`, `/blog`, `/download`, `/login`, `/signup`, `/terms`, `/privacy`

---

## Sitemaps

The sitemap system has 11 named segments (`lib/sitemapData.ts`). When adding new content types:

1. Add a new sitemap ID and handler in `sitemapData.ts`
2. Add the route to `app/sitemap/[id]/route.ts`
3. Set appropriate `priority` and `changefreq`:
   - Homepage: `1.0` / `daily`
   - Core feature/product pages: `0.9` / `weekly`
   - Blog posts: `0.8` / `weekly` (update `lastmod` from post date)
   - Static informational pages: `0.7–0.8` / `monthly`
   - Legal/auth: `0.4–0.6` / `monthly`
4. Use `withLocaleUrls()` for translated pages to include all language variants

---

## OG Images

Dynamic OG images live in `app/api/og/`. When adding a new section with programmatic pages:

1. Create `app/api/og/[section]/route.tsx` using `next/og` (edge runtime)
2. Reference it in `generateMetadata()`:
   ```typescript
   openGraph: {
     images: [`/api/og/my-section?slug=${params.slug}`],
   }
   ```
3. Always provide a 1200×630 fallback if the route fails
4. Preload the wallpaper and Inter font — see existing OG routes for the pattern

---

## FAQ Pages

Any page with an FAQ section should:
1. Render `<FAQAccordion faqs={feature.faqs} />` for the visible UI
2. Inject `generateFAQSchema(feature.faqs)` via `<JsonLd>` for rich snippets
3. Have at least 3 questions. Google won't show the rich snippet for fewer.

---

## noIndex

Use `generatePageMetadata({ noIndex: true })` for:
- Auth pages (`/login`, `/signup`, `/verify-email`)
- Internal/dashboard pages
- Thank-you / success pages
- Any page with `?q=` or pagination query params that duplicate content

Never set `noIndex` on public marketing or programmatic SEO pages.

content.text:
Type-check clean. Lint warnings are pre-existing (unrelated). Here's what shipped:

**Copy** (Platforms.tsx:124):
> "You shouldn't have to come check on me. Tell me where you already hang out and I'll text you — briefings, urgent emails, anything that can't wait."

**Demo card** (new `OnboardingPlatformPreview.tsx`):
- Chat surface only, no phone bezel, sitting inside a `rounded-2xl bg-zinc-900 p-3` card above the connect buttons
- Auto-rotates through Telegram → WhatsApp → Discord, cadence = cascade duration + 1.8s dwell
- Hovering a connect button immediately swaps the demo to that platform and pauses rotation; `onMouseLeave` resumes
- Keyboard accessible via `onFocus` / `onBlur` on the buttons
- Hides once the user connects a platform (so it doesn't compete with the "Connected" confirmation row)

**Profession-keyed messages** (`constants/platformPreviewMessages.ts`):
- 20 professions grouped into 5 archetypes (builder / operator / founder / scholar / default)
- Each archetype has 3 scripts (telegram = morning briefing, whatsapp = urgent reply, discord = workflow finished)
- All in one file — rebucket or rewrite without touching the component

**Landing page safety:**
- The `useStaggeredMessages` cascade hook moved from inline in `BotsShowcaseSection.tsx` into a shared module (`landing/components/iphone/useStaggeredMessages.ts`)
- `BotsShowcaseSection` now imports from there — same cadence (450ms typing delay, 850ms typing duration), same behavior, just sourced from a shared file
- `cascadeDurationMs(n)` helper replaces the inline math at the auto-rotate timer; landing rotation is identical
- Removed the now-duplicate local hook + the now-unused `useMemo` import
- Type-check passes — no changes to `ChatDemo`, `IPhoneMockup`, or any other landing surface

Three small things still open — flagging so you can decide:

1. **The onboarding demo route** (`/onboarding/demo/page.tsx`) — I passed `onHoverPlatform={() => {}}` to satisfy the type. The demo page won't show the preview card. 3. **The earlier API fix** (workflows = 3 enforcement in `intelligence_service.py`) is uncommitted alongside the frontend work. Both are on the same branch — let me know when you want to commit/push.

Otherwise: type-check green, landing page untouched behaviorally, and the preview is wired end-to-end with hover override + profession-keyed scripts.

content.text:
Locked it down properly:

- **Width**: 280px hard-locked via inline style (`width`, `minWidth`, `maxWidth` all = 280). No more shrink on narrow viewports.
- **Inner chat area**: 240px hard-locked the same way (`height`, `minHeight`, `maxHeight`). The motion div inside is now `absolute inset-0` so AnimatePresence's enter/exit can't push the container during platform swaps.
- **Radius bumped to `rounded-3xl`** (outer card) and inner chat to `rounded-2xl` to nest properly.
- **Scaled down for onboarding**: 320→280 width, 280→240 chat height, header strip h-6→h-5, icon 18→14, text-xs→text-[11px], gap-2→gap-1.5, p-3→p-2.5. Sits cleaner inside the bubble.

Dimensions are now invariant across cascade frames and platform switches. Type-check clean.

content.text:
Clean. Summary of this round:

- **`ChatDemo` got a new `showHeader?: boolean` prop** (defaults `true`, so landing untouched). Wired into all 5 variants (iMessage, WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord) — each platform's header block is now gated.
- **`OnboardingPlatformPreview`** passes `showHeader={false}` so the avatar/back-button/channel-name strip is hidden in onboarding. Bumped width 280→360.
- **Fixed formatter collateral**: the biome formatter stripped JSX `{ ... }` braces from existing `showComposer` blocks in iMessage and Discord during my header edits, leaving stray `showComposer && (` and trailing `)` that broke compilation. Restored those.

Type-check green. Landing page behavior unchanged (defaults preserved).

content.text:
Type-check green. Big diff summary:

**Dimensions**: width 460→500, height 320→280. Reduced spacing — preview now sits in a `mt-3` block but the buttons sit at `mt-1` below it (was `mt-3`).

**Auto-scroll**: added a `useLayoutEffect` that finds the `.overflow-y-auto` scroller inside each platform variant and pins it to `scrollHeight` on every visible-message change. New bubbles always stay in view inside the fixed 280px frame.

**Slack added as a 4th platform**:
- `PlatformPreviewPlatform` type extended to `"telegram" | "whatsapp" | "slack" | "discord"`
- Slack in `PLATFORM_LABELS` / `PLATFORM_ICONS` / `PLATFORM_PREVIEW_ORDER`
- Slack button added to `OnboardingPlatformConnect`
- 5 archetype × Slack scripts written (Slack channel titles: eng / leadership / founders / lab / general)

**Discord scripts now include the user**:
Each archetype's Discord script gains a user-attributed bubble between GAIA messages. User replies use placeholder sentinels (`__user__` / `__user_avatar__`) so the renderer can substitute the live identity.

**Live user identity**:
- `getPlatformScript` accepts `{ name, avatar }` and hydrates the placeholders
- `OnboardingPlatformPreview` takes `userName` + `userAvatar` props
- `Platforms.tsx` reads `state.responses.name` (the onboarding answer) with fallback to `useUserStore().name`, and `useUserStore().profilePicture` for the avatar
- Default fallback to `aryan-avatar.webp` only if both store and onboarding name are empty

**Demo route**:
- Production gate: `if (process.env.NODE_ENV === "production") notFound()` at module top — 404s for normal users, dev/preview still works
- Added platform chips (Telegram / WhatsApp / Slack / Discord) — clicking pins the preview to that platform, clicking again unpins
- Added profession chips (first 10 from `professionOptions`) so you can flip archetype and see message variants
- Reads the real user from `useUserStore` so the Discord/Slack user-bubbles show your actual name + avatar

lastPrompt: does the onboarding page hide the header??

content.text:
Short answer: **partially.** Here's what actually renders on `/onboarding`:

- **`HeaderManager.tsx:48-65`** has no case for `/onboarding`, so it returns `null` — no feature header content (no breadcrumbs, no page title, no actions).
- **But the wrapping `<header>` element in `(main)/layout.tsx:145-149` still renders** unconditionally, containing the `HeaderSidebarTrigger` (sidebar toggle button). So a thin top bar with just the hamburger sits above the onboarding chat.
- **The sidebar itself also renders** — `(main)/layout.tsx:139-141` mounts `<Sidebar />` for every route under `(main)/`, with no onboarding bypass.

So the "page header" (titles/actions) is hidden, but the layout chrome (header bar + sidebar toggle + sidebar) is not. If you want a truly clean onboarding canvas, the layout needs a pathname check that skips the `<header>` block (and optionally the sidebar) when `pathname.startsWith("/onboarding")`.

Want me to add that gate?

1	/**

4	 * into 5 archetypes; each archetype owns three scripts (one per platform)
5	 * that demonstrate the three things the copy promises: morning briefings,
6	 * urgent email flags, and workflow-finished pings.
7	 *
8	 * Rebucket professions or rewrite scripts here, the preview component is
9	 * data-driven and has no other coupling to profession.
10	 */
11

20	>;

23	  | "builder"
24	  | "operator"
25	  | "founder"
26	  | "scholar"
27	  | "default";

30	  engineer: "builder",

32	  designer: "builder",
33	
34	  manager: "operator",
35	  consultant: "operator",

49	  writer: "scholar",
50	
51	  artist: "default",
52	  retired: "default",
53	  other: "default",
54	};
55	
56	export function getArchetype(
57	  profession: string | undefined,
58	): ProfessionArchetype {
59	  if (!profession) return "default";
60	  return PROFESSION_TO_ARCHETYPE[profession.toLowerCase()] ?? "default";
61	}

content.text:
Type-check clean. Recap of this round:

- **No em dashes in demo scripts** — all 21 occurrences in `platformPreviewMessages.ts` swapped to commas (external edit confirmed). The only remaining em-dash in the onboarding stage is inside the bubble copy itself, which you explicitly included.
- **Bubble copy updated** to your exact text with curly quotes (`’`).
- **Order**: preview → bubble → buttons. All three are now siblings of the stage's flex column with `gap-3`. The bubble no longer wraps the buttons.
- **Buttons swapped to `RaisedButton color="black"`** — the black flat treatment (gradient `#444→#000`, subtle border, white text). Icon size dropped 26→22 to match the raised button proportions.
